interpretation: Endothelial VWF-SELP suggests a Weibel-Palade body/P-selectin-like vascular adhesion state.
evidence_note: VWF and P-selectin are core endothelial Weibel-Palade body cargos linked to hemostasis, inflammation, and leukocyte rolling.

interpretation: Endothelial VWF toward CAF LRP1 highlights a vascular-to-stromal matrix/scavenger-receptor interface.
evidence_note: This is a high-scoring dataset-supported vascular-stromal hypothesis rather than a single canonical pathway claim.
reference: Interpreted as ECM/scavenger-receptor biology in the pyXenium WTA context; use as dataset-supported hypothesis.

interpretation: Endothelial EFNB2-PECAM1 supports a vascular identity and endothelial adhesion/state axis.
evidence_note: Both genes mark vascular/endothelial biology; interpret as endothelial organization rather than secreted paracrine signaling.
reference: Reactome VEGFA-VEGFR2 pathway and VEGF-family angiogenesis context: https://reactome.org/content/detail/R-HSA-4420097

interpretation: MMRN2-CLEC14A points to tumor endothelial matrix remodeling and angiogenic vessel state.
evidence_note: CD93/CLEC14A/MMRN2 family biology is linked to tumor vasculature, matrix organization, and endothelial angiogenic programs.
reference: CD93 promotes beta1 integrin activation and fibronectin fibrillogenesis during tumor angiogenesis: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6063507/

interpretation: Endothelial HSPG2 to CAF LRP1 suggests basement-membrane-rich vascular-stromal remodeling.
evidence_note: LRP1 frequently acts as a scavenger/ECM-associated receptor; here the strongest support is spatial WTA context.
reference: Interpreted as ECM/scavenger-receptor biology in the pyXenium WTA context; use as dataset-supported hypothesis.

interpretation: COL4A2-CD93 links endothelial basement membrane signal with angiogenic endothelial receptor state.
evidence_note: CD93 is implicated in endothelial matrix organization and tumor angiogenesis.
reference: CD93 promotes beta1 integrin activation and fibronectin fibrillogenesis during tumor angiogenesis: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6063507/

interpretation: MMRN2-CD93 is a direct interpretable tumor-endothelial matrix/angiogenesis axis.
evidence_note: CD93-MMRN2 signaling has reported roles in beta1 integrin activation and fibronectin fibrillogenesis during tumor angiogenesis.
reference: CD93 promotes beta1 integrin activation and fibronectin fibrillogenesis during tumor angiogenesis: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6063507/

interpretation: VWF-ITGA9 extends the VWF vascular adhesion theme within endothelial neighborhoods.
evidence_note: Interpret as an integrin-associated vascular adhesion hypothesis supported by endothelial spatial contact.

interpretation: CAF MMP2 toward endothelial PECAM1 supports a stromal-remodeling interface around vasculature.
evidence_note: MMP2 is a classic ECM remodeling enzyme; PECAM1 anchors the receiver identity as endothelial.
reference: Interpreted as ECM/scavenger-receptor biology in the pyXenium WTA context; use as dataset-supported hypothesis.

interpretation: T-cell CD48-CD2 indicates lymphocyte-local adhesion/co-stimulation biology.
evidence_note: CD48-CD2 has experimental support in T-cell adhesion and activation contexts.
reference: Enhanced murine CD4+ T cell responses induced by the CD2 ligand CD48: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/9862369/

interpretation: VEGFC-FLT1 supports a vascular growth-factor/angiogenesis interpretation.
evidence_note: Canonical VEGF-family ligand/receptor biology makes this a straightforward angiogenic axis.
reference: Reactome VEGFA-VEGFR2 pathway and VEGF-family angiogenesis context: https://reactome.org/content/detail/R-HSA-4420097

interpretation: Endothelial DLL4 to pericyte NOTCH3 supports vascular stabilization and pericyte-endothelial Notch biology.
evidence_note: Notch1/Notch3 and DLL4-linked endothelial-pericyte signaling are reported in vascular stabilization contexts.
reference: Notch1 and Notch3 coordinate for pericyte-induced stabilization of vasculature: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/34878922/

interpretation: CAF CXCL12 to T-cell CXCR4 recovers a canonical stromal chemokine immune-recruitment axis.
evidence_note: CXCL12/CXCR4 is a well-studied cancer-stroma chemokine pathway with immune and tumor-microenvironment relevance.

interpretation: CAF THBS2 to endothelial CD36 supports matrix-rich stromal angiogenesis biology.
evidence_note: THBS/CD36 biology is interpretable in matrix remodeling, angiogenesis, and stromal context.
reference: Interpreted as ECM/scavenger-receptor biology in the pyXenium WTA context; use as dataset-supported hypothesis.

sender_receiver: 11q13 Invasive Tumor Cells -> 11q13 Invasive Tumor Cells
interpretation: Tumor-intrinsic JAG1-NOTCH2 suggests a Notch signaling state within 11q13 invasive tumor cells.
evidence_note: JAG1/Notch signaling is broadly linked to cancer cell state, angiogenesis, stemness, EMT, and therapy resistance.
reference: The Notch ligand Jagged1 as a target for anti-tumor therapy: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4174884/
